title: Classification of Systems
summary: This page discusses time-invariant and time-varying systems, providing examples and a formal example (1.11) that assesses time invariance for two systems: y(t)=x(t)u(t) (shown to be time-variant via counterexample) and y(t)=d/dt x(t) (shown to be time-invariant). It also introduces the distinction between instantaneous and dynamic systems.
